vision.title: Vision
vision.content: To become a leading innovator in enzyme-based solutions that transform agriculture into a cleaner, more sustainable, and more productive ecosystem across Vietnam and the region.

mission.title: Mission
mission.content: BISO brings enzyme technology closer to farmers, helping improve soil health, enhance crop productivity, and produce safe agricultural products for the community; while also reducing the use of chemical fertilizers and harmful substances, thereby protecting farmers’ health and the environment.
commitments: Work closely with farmers and farming communities
Build a clean agricultural ecosystem with transparent traceability

items.title: Science-driven innovation
items.desc: We believe science and biotechnology are the foundation for agricultural innovation.

items.title: Harmony with nature
items.desc: We develop solutions that work in balance with the soil, water, and crop ecosystem.

items.title: Farmer-first mindset
items.desc: BISO’s success is closely tied to the sustainable growth of farmers.

items.title: Integrity & transparency
items.desc: We prioritize transparency, traceability, and responsibility to the community.

items.title: Innovation for the future
items.desc: We continuously seek new solutions to elevate Vietnamese agriculture.

bisoyai.title: BISO Young Agri Innovators 2026
bisoyai.desc: More than an internship — this is a journey to shape the next generation of farmers. Learn enzyme technology from the ground up and master clean agriculture with BISO.

leaders.name: Mr. Ngo Khanh Linh
leaders.title: Founder & Chairman of the Members' Council
points: A pioneer in Vietnam's circular agriculture sector, Mr. Linh has over 15 years of experience consulting and implementing organic farming models, carbon emission reduction, and agricultural land regulations.

As Founder and Chairman, Mr. Linh is the driving force behind the strategy to import and apply Japanese microbial enzyme technology in real-world farming — contributing to soil restoration, yield improvement, and sustainable agricultural production.
Mr. Linh also advises on international standards such as USDA Organic, GlobalG.A.P., and GACC, helping agribusinesses access export markets and carbon credit opportunities.

leaders.name: Ms. Nguyen Phuoc Huong Thao
leaders.title: Chief Executive Officer
points: Ms. Hương Thảo has held senior management positions at KFC, Starbucks, Dairy Queen, The Coffee House, The Coffee Bean & Tea Leaf, MK Restaurant, and Food Court systems.
She currently serves as General Manager of VFBS, overseeing the Popeyes Vietnam brand — the globally renowned American fried chicken chain.

Ms. Hương Thảo is recognized as a strategic expert with a proven ability to restructure operations, build high-performing teams, and scale systems — particularly in international franchise projects and F&B market development in Vietnam.

With over 20 years of cross-industry management experience spanning F&B, real estate, printing, and agriculture, she consistently partners with businesses to create sustainable value and long-term growth.

partner1.heading: 1. Member of the Vietnam Organic Agriculture Association

partner5.caption: Partnering with Dr. Phung Ha, President of the Vietnam Fertilizer Association (FAV), Term VI (2024-2029)

partner6.heading: 6. Partnering with the Hai Duong Provincial Farmers' Union

news.desc: Explore the application of biological enzyme technology in agriculture, industry, and aquaculture — the foundation for a greener and more sustainable Vietnam.
news.footer: Biological Enzymes · Japanese Technology · USDA · GlobalG.A.P.

articles.title: Biological Enzyme Technology – A Sustainable Solution for Vietnamese Agriculture
articles.excerpt: As Vietnamese agriculture faces soil degradation and rising pest pressure, biological enzymes have emerged as a comprehensive solution for soil restoration meeting USDA and GlobalG.A.P. international standards.

articles.title: Biological Enzymes in Industry – Effective Environmental Treatment Solutions
articles.excerpt: From BOD/COD wastewater treatment and NH₃/H₂S odor elimination in farms, to biological detergents — enzymes deliver a comprehensive green solution for businesses on their sustainable transition journey.

articles.title: Biological Solutions for Aquaculture – Improving Farming Efficiency
articles.excerpt: Biological enzymes help balance pond environments, decompose toxic gases H₂S and NH₃, and naturally boost immunity in shrimp and fish — aiming for international export standards without relying on antibiotics.

articles.title: Enzyme: The Biological "Medicine" Saving Soil and Reviving Gardens Thought Dead
articles.excerpt: Degraded, hardened soil, stunted roots, widespread pests — this is the common reality in many long-farmed areas. Despite heavy fertilizing and chemical inputs, farmers watch helplessly as their gardens wither.

articles.title: Visiting Rare Plants on Kim Cuong Island – BISO JICA Visits Mr. Vinh's Date Palm Garden
articles.excerpt: Welcome to the BISO JICA channel – where we share knowledge and solutions on organic enzymes applied in agriculture, industry, and aquaculture.

articles.title: Clean and Eco-Friendly Solutions for Agriculture and Waste Management
articles.excerpt: Numerous solutions for water treatment, wastewater processing, odor control, and agricultural soil improvement are garnering significant interest from businesses and farmers in their production activities.

blogAgri.subtitle: A Sustainable Solution for Vietnamese Agriculture
blogAgri.intro: As Vietnamese agriculture faces soil degradation, rising pest pressure, and export standard demands, biological enzyme technology has emerged as a comprehensive and sustainable solution.

section1.title: Superior Biological Effectiveness
section1.desc: Biological enzymes are developed to suit Vietnam's climate, soil conditions, and farming practices. Through their natural mechanism of action, the products deliver outstanding benefits.
section1.quote: Beyond helping crops grow healthier, biological solutions also improve soil microbial ecosystems — the core foundation of sustainable agriculture.

features.text: Break down organic residues and chemicals
Support control of fungal diseases and pests

section2.title: Absolutely Safe for the Ecosystem
section2.desc: Unlike conventional agricultural chemicals, biological enzymes are designed with maximum safety standards.
points: 100% biodegradable
No harmful residues
Safe for users and livestock
Does not pollute water or soil

section3.title: Internationally Certified
section3.desc: Built on Japanese technology, our products meet the stringent standards of global markets.
section3.quote: Using biological enzymes not only improves farming efficiency but also expands access to global markets.

blogIndustry.intro: Industrial pollution is becoming an urgent issue. From wastewater and emissions to odors, businesses need solutions that are both effective and environmentally friendly.

sections.title: BOD & COD Wastewater Treatment
sections.body: Biological enzymes break down organic compounds, reduce BOD and COD levels, and improve biological treatment system efficiency. Through their natural catalytic mechanism, treatment is faster, operating costs are reduced, and chemical dependency is minimized.

sections.title: Odor Elimination in Farms & Landfills
sections.body: Odors from toxic gases like NH₃ and H₂S not only affect the environment but also negatively impact public health. Biological enzymes decompose toxic gases, effectively reduce odors, and improve working environments.

sections.title: Eco-Friendly Biological Detergents
sections.body: In industrial production, enzymes are applied in biological detergent formulations. Effective cleaning, non-corrosive, no water pollution — an inevitable trend in the green transition.

callout.title: An Inevitable Trend
callout.desc: This is the inevitable trend in the green transition and sustainable development of Vietnamese businesses in the new era.

blogAquaculture.intro: Vietnam's aquaculture industry plays a vital role in exports, but also faces many challenges such as pond pollution, toxic gases, and disease outbreaks.

benefits.title: Improve Pond Environment
benefits.note: Clean ponds help shrimp and fish develop steadily and reduce loss rates.

benefits.title: Reduce Toxic Gases H₂S, NH₃
benefits.note: Toxic gases are the main cause of stress and disease in aquatic species.

benefits.title: Boost Immunity in Shrimp & Fish
benefits.note: An important step towards sustainable aquaculture.

why.title: Why did BISO create this program?
why.line2: But sustainable change doesn't start from products. It starts from mindset.
why.quote: "BISO believes: If agriculture students can access enzyme technology and clean farming zone models while still in school, they will become the next generation of innovative engineers and farmers."

experience.footer: This is not an "office-sitting" internship. This is real hands-on practice.

page1.title: Enzyme – The Invisible Foundation of Sustainable Agriculture

page2.title: Enzymes – The Catalysts of Life
page2.intro: Enzymes are like "scissors and keys" in nature. They help to:
points: Cut, break down, and transform nutrients into forms that plants can easily absorb.
Unlock and activate the life processes of plants and microorganisms.
page2.example1: Example: If nutrients in the soil are the "food", then enzymes are the "cooking pot" – helping to "cook" them so plants can consume them.
page2.example2: Without enzymes → even nutrient-rich soil cannot be effectively absorbed by plants.
page2.footer: Without enzymes, life could not be sustained – even for us humans.

sections.heading: Loosen soil – increase beneficial microorganisms
points: Break down crop residues and organic matter faster.
Improve soil quality and reduce root rot and yellowing diseases.

sections.heading: Strengthen plant resistance
points: Plants with sufficient enzymes = stronger immunity.
Better resistance to pests, diseases, and harsh weather.
Extend the shelf life of agricultural products by slowing down ripening, decay, and oxidation after harvest.

sections.heading: Improve nutrient absorption
points: Release nutrients that are "locked" in the soil.
Plants feed better, grow evenly, and require less fertilizer.

sections.heading: Reduce chemical usage
points: Naturally stronger plants with fewer diseases.
Reduce spraying of pesticides and chemical fertilizers.
Bind heavy metals in the soil, preventing crops from being affected by toxins from chemical fertilizers, pesticides, and environmental pollution.

page4.title: So What Is the Miracle Enzyme?
page4.body1: "Miracle Enzyme" refers to the prototype enzyme that serves as the foundation for more than 5,000 other enzymes in living organisms. It is the "root" behind the biological reactions that sustain life.
page4.body2: Miracle enzymes are not only beneficial for human health but also have remarkable applications in sustainable agriculture. They help protect the environment, improve soil quality, and enhance crop productivity in a natural and efficient way. By using them, farmers can reduce the use of chemical fertilizers and pesticides, creating a healthier agricultural ecosystem for both people and nature.

page5.title: Enzymes vs. Miracle Enzymes – What’s the Difference?

rows.label: Nature
rows.miracle: A prototype enzyme – the origin of thousands of other enzymes.

rows.label: Role
rows.normal: Performs one specific task (e.g., digesting starch).
rows.miracle: Activates and regulates a wide range of biological reactions.

rows.label: Impact
rows.normal: Localized, based on a specific function.
rows.miracle: Comprehensive – from absorption and detoxification to immunity.

rows.label: In agriculture
rows.normal: Supports decomposition, soil improvement, and nutrient absorption.
rows.miracle: Improves soil, stimulates microorganisms, boosts immunity, and reduces pesticide usage.

rows.label: Example
rows.miracle: A chief engineer operating the entire system.

page6.title: Why Use Enzymes (Especially Miracle Enzymes)?
points: An "invisible assistant" that helps soil become loose and fertile while improving nutrient absorption in plants.
Like the plant’s "digestive enzyme" – helping crops convert nutrients and grow naturally stronger.
Supports flowering and fruiting while enhancing resistance to pests and diseases.
Promotes strong root development and overall plant growth.
Reduces soil and water pollution while maintaining biological balance in natural agriculture.
Transforms agricultural residues such as straw and bagasse into bioethanol – producing clean energy from farm by-products.
Moves toward green and sustainable agriculture – lowering costs while increasing farmers’ profits.
page6.quote: "Soil with enzymes is like a field with earthworms – living soil, healthy crops, and less need for chemical spraying!"
